source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the picture $A B C D$ is a square of side 1 and the semicircles have centers on $A, B, C$ and $D$. What is the length of $P Q$?
<image1>

image_dependency: points P and Q are not defined in the text alone, so the diagram is necessary
geometry depends on visual placement of a square and semicircles
multi_step: solving for PQ likely requires combining diagram interpretation with geometric relationships
the setup includes multiple objects: square ABCD and semicircles centered at A, B, C, and D
answer is an exact radical expression, suggesting nontrivial derivation rather than direct reading
verifiability: provided answer is a precise symbolic value: sqrt(3)-1
multiple-choice options are available, enabling answer cross-checking

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image_dependency_score and existing diagram are consistent, so the image-based geometry can be recovered
high verifiability_score with a precise short-text answer supports reliable downstream checking
although point definitions depend on the diagram, this is not a blocking issue because the required image asset exists
